rationale: This page provides detailed information on the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act (FCRA), 2010, including Section 7, general provisions, and the 2020 amendments. It includes a list of entities prohibited from receiving foreign funds and administrative requirements for NGOs. It also briefly mentions the Swadhar Greh and Ujjwala schemes.
cleanedText: Swadhar Greh Scheme: Provides institutional support for rehabilitation of women who faced crime, violence, mental stress so that they could lead their life with dignity. Ujjwala scheme: Prevent trafficking of women and children for commercial sexual exploitation through social mobilization. 7.8. FOREIGN CONTRIBUTION (REGULATION) ACT, 2010. Why in the news? Recently, Supreme Court pulled up the Union Government for non-granting of FCRA renewal for two NGOs due to violation of Section 7 of FCRA 2010. About Section 7 of FCRA 2010: It prohibits any person or organization that has received foreign contribution under the Act from transferring such funds to any other person or organization. Exception: Contributions made by Non-Resident Indian, from his personal savings, through the normal banking channels, is not treated as foreign contribution. About Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act (FCRA) 2010: Foreign contribution means donation, delivery or transfer made by any foreign source of any article, currency, or security. FCRA is implemented by MHA, supported by Intelligence Bureau in approvals and rejections through investigation on antecedents. Major Provisions of FCRA Post 2020 amendment: 1. Foreign funds: Prior Permission to receive foreign funds though registration with mandatory Aadhaar submission of every office-bearer of NGOs. Prohibits receipt of foreign funds. Who can not receive Foreign Contributions? Candidates for Elections, Judges and Government servants, Journalists or newspaper and media broadcast companies, Member of Legislature and political parties or their office bearers, Organisations of a political nature. 2. FCRA Account: Designated FCRA Account in such branches of State Bank of India, New Delhi to receive funds. No funds other than foreign contribution can be received or deposited in it and it can't be transferred to any other person or NGO. 3. Validity of FCRA registration: Validity is five years and NGOs are expected to apply for renewal within six months of date of expiry of registration. 4. Reduction in use of foreign contribution: Use of funds is limited to purpose for which it was received with a maximum limit of 20% (earlier 50%) to meet administrative expenses. 5. Submission of annual reports: Compulsory filing of annual returns and in case of contraventions, government can restrict usage of unutilized foreign contribution after an inquiry.
